user_input: Run a deep summary of NVDA news and tell me the dominant sentiment.

arguments.task: Summarize the last 7 days of NVDA news and surface dominant sentiment.

final_answer: Subagent reports neutral-to-positive sentiment on NVDA over the last 7 days, dominated by earnings-beat coverage.
notes: Hand-crafted subagent fixture. Parent anchors delegate_to_subagent — this is the spec's core Phase C failure mode (a refactor that drops the bridge-side delegate_to_subagent without registering a replacement must fail this fixture). Parent's pinned_tool_names = ['delegate_to_subagent'] is a REQUIRED-RESOLUTION list, not a skip-list: commit 3's validator must resolve every pinned name through a unified resolver that consults ToolRegistry FIRST, then server:* tools via shared/server_tools.py, then provider bridge surfaces (Anthropic + OpenAI) for bridge-only tools like delegate_to_subagent. Pinning never means 'trust and skip lookup' — that would let Phase C drop delegate_to_subagent silently. The child trace embedded in subagent_traces[] mirrors what the data_summarizer subagent actually invoked; commit 3 is expected to recurse into the child's tool_calls and pin its own dependency (get_ticker_news) at child level via the same unified resolver. Hashes are illustrative; the field structure + resolution semantics are what's load-bearing for the validator.
